dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 10, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**MLB Recap: April 30th Highlights**

The Marlins' offense roared to life in a 5-2 win over the Nationals, led by Heriberto Hernández's 2-for-4 performance with 2 RBIs. Hernández's clutch single in the 5th inning scored two crucial runs for Miami.

On the pitching side, Andrew Nardi made a statement with 2 strikeouts in just 0.2 innings of work for the Marlins. Andrew Abbott also turned heads with 5 strikeouts in 6 innings of shutout ball for the Reds in a 5-0 win over the Astros.

In the Angels' 6-1 victory over the Blue Jays, Jo Adell crushed 2 home runs, driving in 2 runs and leading Los Angeles to a dominant win. Meanwhile, José Soriano tossed 7.2 scoreless innings for the Angels, fanning 7 and limiting the Blue Jays to just 5 hits.

Kyle Schwarber's 2-home run, 3-hit performance helped the Phillies blank the Rockies 6-0. Cristopher Sánchez pitched a gem for Philadelphia, allowing just 6 hits and 0 runs in 7 innings of work.

Aaron Judge's 3-run homer was not enough for the Yankees, who fell 4-3 to the Brewers in a back-and-forth contest. Brice Turang homered for Milwaukee, driving in the winning run in the 8th inning.

In a thrilling game, the Giants outlasted the Pirates 7-6, with Heliot Ramos crushing a home run and driving in 2 runs.
